briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — aggressive post-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for post-spawn with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on point, creek channel, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

KVD: Dice/bug baits on a drop shot are a new frontier. Lighter weight = better (1/8oz ideal, 1/4oz max in deeper water). The slow sinking action is the key — once on bottom you can still shake it traditionally, but most bites come on the fall. Gam...
Yamamoto pro Fukae: Rigging a 3-inch Fat Senko Texas-rigged on a drop-shot with a 3-inch leader replicates a Ned Rig presentation but avoids bottom hang-ups around cover

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal post-spawn bass are transitioning and aggressive. Males guard fry in 2-6ft while females roam 5-12ft feeding heavily. Target both shallow fry-guarders and roaming post-spawn females near points.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
